Catalog Description

Design and Analysis of Industrial Systems with Simulation. (2-3). Credit 3. In-depth

study into the design-modeling and subsequent analysis of contemporary

production/service systems; factory/service systems are modeled using the

ARENA/SIMAN V simulation-animation language; emphasis is placed on the critical

analysis of alternative flow designs of modeled systems using flow and economic

parameters to assess system improvement. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in

ISEN 210 and ISEN 355.

Additional Course Details

There is an approved change to the catalog description to reflect the move away

from the ARENA/SIMAN V simulation-automation language. The revised catalog

description is stated below:

In-depth study into the design-modeling and subsequent analysis of contemporary

production/service systems; factory/service systems are modeled using a modern

simulation software; emphasis is placed on the critical analysis of alternative flow

designs of modeled systems using flow and economic parameters to assess system

improvement.

We will be using the Simio simulation software in this course as a continuation of

the software used in the prerequisite course ISEN 355.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in ISEN 210 and ISEN 355.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

develop and analyze complex models for industrial engineering problems

using commercially available discrete event simulation software,

interpret simulation output using valid statistical methods and make

appropriate recommendations, and

analyze data to determine appropriate input distributions using valid

statistical methods.

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

Grade Components and Weights

There are three exams in the course. There will be weekly lab assignments, some

graded homework, regular quizzes, and a project.

Exam 1 and Exam 2 are each worth 10%. The lab exam is worth 30%.

The lab assignments together will be worth 15%. Submissions will not be

accepted after the deadline with the exception of a university excused

absence.

There will be regular quizzes during the semester. The weight of each quiz is

dependent on the number of questions in the quiz. All the quizzes together

will be worth 10%. Quizzes must be taken in the classroom during the

designated quiz time.

Attempting a quiz outside of the classroom is not permitted and will

be treated as a violation of the Aggie Honor Code.

Some of the homework will be collected and graded. The homework grade is

worth 5%.

The project will be worth 20%. More details about the project will be provided

during the semester.

Grading Policy

Grades assigned are: A for 90% - 100%, B for 80% - 89.9%, C for 70% - 79.9%, D for

60% - 69.9%, F for below 60%. Any possibility of a curve on grades will be

announced in the last few days of the semester.

Any disagreements regarding a grade received on any graded material must be

discussed within two business days of the return of the graded material.  No grade

will be changed beyond the two business days limit.

Grades will be maintained in the gradebook within Canvas. Please note that the

announced in the last few days of the semester.

Any disagreements regarding a grade received on any graded material must be

discussed within two business days of the return of the graded material.  No grade

will be changed beyond the two business days limit.

Grades will be maintained in the gradebook within Canvas. Please note that the

automated total and grade calculations on the Canvas gradebook are useless, and

you should not rely on them. Use your engineering/math skills to compute your

score based on the weights described in the  Grade Components and Weights

section above.

Note About Homework

There will be homework assignments that will be assigned regularly. Some of the

homework will be collected and graded. It is in your interest to work on the

homework problems and develop the solutions. The solutions will be posted  for

you to compare your work. You can expect to see quiz questions and exam

problems based on the homework problems.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late work is not accepted in this course.

Additional Course Information

Copyright Notice

All documents used in this course are copyrighted. Here, "documents" means all

materials generated for this class, including, but not limited to, syllabi, quizzes,

exams, problem sets, slide sets, and all materials appearing on CANVAS or sent to

you via email. Because these materials are copyrighted, you do not have the right

to copy any of them for any purpose other than your own personal academic use

unless I expressly grant permission. In particular, course materials may not be

given or sold to any non-profit or for-profit enterprise.

Software

The Industrial and Systems Engineering Department uses Simio under a grant from

Simio LLC ( https://www.simio.com ). Although Simio is installed in the ISEN Cloud, I

strongly recommend that you install a copy of the software on your PC. You will

find the detailed instructions on Canvas and the Technology Support portion of this

syllabus. You will likely find this advantageous for working at home and in the

classroom.

Simio does not work with the Mac Operating System (MacOS), but some students

with Macs indicated that Simio worked well using Bootcamp, but I have no personal

experience with using Simio on a Mac. Simio has provided some helpful resources

for Mac users. This can be found at   https://www.simio.com/publications/simio-

for-mac/ .

Simio is available in the Engineering Desktop available through the Aggie Virtual

Desktop – see  https://service.tamu.edu/TDClient/36/Portal/KB/ArticleDet?ID=657

for more information.

Expectations

Students are expected to attend all class and lab meetings.

Students should come prepared to the class and lab meetings whenever

there are any assigned reading or review assignments.

Students are expected to bring their own computer for every class and lab

meeting.

Missed work due to unexcused absence will not be accepted.

Class Communication

Communication and announcements with the class will be maintained using email

and Canvas.

Most of the class communication will be via email through the TAMUDirect

mailing list maintained by the university. Students are expected to carefully

read all the emails in their entirety.

AI Statement

According to the Texas A&M University Definitions of Academic Misconduct,

plagiarism is the appropriation of another person's ideas, processes, results or

words without giving appropriate credit ( aggiehonor.tamu.edu ). You should credit

your use of anyone else's words, graphic images, or ideas using standard citation

styles. Artificial Intelligence (AI) text generators and natural language processing

tools (colloquially, chatbots - such as ChatGPT), audio, computer code, video, and

image generators  should not be used for any work for this class without

explicit permission of the instructor  and appropriate attribution. This includes,

but is not limited to,

i. Creating or revising drafts

ii. Editing your work

iii. Reviewing a peer's work

This excludes pre-existing software additions such as spelling and grammar

checkers, which are acceptable.

Course Specific Attendance Policy

Students are expected to attend class in person and to complete all assignments.

Lab assignment and homework submissions and attempting a quiz after the posted

deadline will not be accepted unless there is a university excused absence.

Please note that the rule regarding job interviews is as follows: “Mandatory

interviews for permanent, full-time employment or full-time internships (including

those that are part of a cooperative education program) that have a duration of at

least 10 weeks, provided that such interviews are related to the student’s

academic program and provided that the interviews are fixed date by employer

policy and cannot be rescheduled. A student may not request excused absences for

employment or internship interviews for more than one scheduled class meeting in

one academic term.” Also, “Documentation from the employer must be provided 5

business days in advance.”

Course Specific Makeup Work Policy

Students are expected to attend class in person and to complete all assignments.

Lab assignment and homework submissions and attempting a quiz after the posted

deadline will not be accepted unless there is a university excused absence.

Make-up exam will be only for students that have provided documentation

supporting their absence in accordance with the excused absence policy described

in Student Rule 7.
